title: React Router Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of client-side routing, BrowserRouter, Link component, and dynamic route paths.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary role of the React Router in a React app?
options: To sync the UI with a server's URL.
To enable navigation and client-side routing within a single-page application.
To provide a replacement for CSS.
To speed up the app by caching components.

questions.question: Which component should you use for internal navigation in React Router that avoids a full page reload?
Which component wraps your entire application to enable all routing capabilities?
How do you define a dynamic route for a user profile in React Router (e.g., /user/john)?
React Router is a built-in library in React and doesn't require an external package installation.
